Explore

Discover how developers use AI coding assistants

{conversations?.map(conv => ( ))}
); } ``` **ConversationCard:** - Title (linked to /share/[token]) - Author name + avatar - Preview text (truncated) - Tags as badges - View count, message count - Agent type icon - Time ago **Depends On:** ccs-o9tp (discoverable shares)

title: code-chat-sync: Coding Agent History Sync
description:
A utility that watches Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Cursor history files and pushes conversations to a shared Convex database. Includes background daemon for real-time sync and web dashboard for browsing conversations.

See app_spec.txt for full specification.

Key components:
- CLI daemon (Bun/Node.js) for watching history files
- Convex backend for data storage and real-time sync
- Next.js web dashboard for viewing conversations

Phase 1 (MVP):
- Claude Code support
- Codex CLI support
- Basic web dashboard (view-only)
- Convex Auth
- Personal + team conversation views

Phase 2 (Future):
- Cursor support
- Analytics
- Annotations

title: Mobile: Push notifications
description:
Push notifications for mobile app:

**Infrastructure:**
- Expo Push Notifications
- Register device token with backend
- Store tokens in Convex (user_devices table)

**Notification Types:**
- Session became active
- Session completed
- Agent error
- Permission requested (Tier 2)
- Team mention
- Comment on your conversation

**Server-Side Trigger:**
- Convex action triggered on events
- Send push via Expo Push API
- Include deep link to relevant screen

**Client Handling:**
- Foreground: show in-app banner
- Background: native notification
- Tap: navigate to relevant screen

**Settings:**
- Per-type enable/disable
- Quiet hours option
- Test notification button

**Data Model:**
```typescript
user_devices: defineTable({
    user_id: v.id('users'),
    push_token: v.string(),
    platform: v.union(v.literal('ios'), v.literal('android')),
    created_at: v.number(),
    last_used_at: v.number(),
})
```

title: Deep Git Integration - diff viewer, time-travel, code reconstruction
description:
Full git integration for understanding code changes during agent sessions:

**Core Concept:**
At each point where the agent makes a code change, we capture the git state and can reconstruct/view the actual diffs.

**Phase 1: Capture (Daemon)**
- On each Edit/Write tool completion, run git diff to capture actual changes
- Store commit hash at conversation start
- Store incremental diffs with each tool result
- Track: file path, before/after content, line numbers

**Phase 2: Display (Web)**
- Split view: conversation on left, diff viewer on right
- Click on any Edit/Write tool call to see the diff at that point
- Syntax-highlighted diff view (Monaco editor or similar)
- Navigate forward/backward through changes
- 'Time travel' slider to see code state at any point

**Phase 3: Reconstruction**
- Given starting commit + sequence of diffs, reconstruct file state
- Show full file with changes highlighted
- Support multi-file changes

**Data Model:**
- Store diffs in messages.tool_results or separate diffs table
- Include: file_path, old_content, new_content, line_range, commit_hash

**Reference:** Similar to GitHub PR diff view but temporal/sequential

This is a differentiating feature - seeing exactly what the agent changed.

title: Optional E2E encryption (Enterprise)
description:
Zero-knowledge encryption as optional enterprise feature.

**Concept:**
Like Happy Coder, server never sees plaintext conversation data.

**Architecture:**
- Master secret derived from user password or generated
- Per-session encryption keys
- Keys encrypted with user's master key
- All content encrypted client-side before Convex

**Implementation:**
- Port ~/src/happy/sources/encryption/ patterns
- Key derivation (BIP32-style)
- AES-256 for content
- Box encryption for key exchange

**Trade-offs:**
- More complex
- No server-side search
- Key management UX
- Recovery challenges

**When:**
- After core features stable
- Enterprise customer demand
- Optional toggle, not default

**Reference:** ~/src/happy/sources/sync/encryption/

title: Migrate to shadcn/ui component library
description:
Add shadcn/ui as the component foundation:

**Why:**
- Consistent, accessible components
- Tailwind-native (matches our stack)
- Copy-paste ownership (not npm dependency)
- Radix primitives for complex interactions
- Great dark/light mode support

**Components to add:**
- Button, Input, Textarea (forms)
- Dialog, Sheet, Popover (overlays)
- DropdownMenu, ContextMenu (menus)
- Tabs, Accordion (navigation)
- Card, Badge, Avatar (display)
- Toast, Alert (feedback)
- Command (for Cmd+K search)
- ScrollArea (for virtualized lists)

**Setup:**
npx shadcn-ui@latest init
- Configure tailwind.config.ts
- Set up CSS variables for theming
- Add components incrementally

**Migration:**
- Replace custom components with shadcn equivalents
- Keep custom tool visualization components
- Use shadcn Dialog for search modal
- Use shadcn Command for Cmd+K

Note: shadcn uses Radix UI primitives underneath for accessibility

title: One-command install flow (curl | sh)
description:
Create frictionless installation:
- curl -fsSL codecast.sh/install | sh
- Downloads and installs CLI binary
- Works on macOS (Intel + Apple Silicon) and Linux
- No npm/node dependency for end users
- Binary self-contained with Bun compile
- After install, immediately runs 'codecast setup'
- Installer script hosted at /install route

title: Auto-auth handoff - CLI opens browser, receives token automatically
description:
Seamless authentication without copy-paste:
1. CLI generates ephemeral token/nonce
2. Opens browser to /auth/cli?token=xxx
3. User logs in or signs up in browser
4. Browser completes auth, posts back to localhost callback OR polls endpoint
5. CLI receives auth token automatically
6. Shows 'Authenticated\! Starting sync...'
7. Daemon auto-starts immediately after auth
8. No manual token copying required

Consider: polling endpoint vs localhost callback server

1. Call addMessage with conversation_id, role, content
2. Verify message record created
3. Verify conversation.message_count incremented
4. Verify conversation.updated_at updated

**Test status:** passing
**attempt_count:** 1

title: Team Features Epic - invite, profiles, activity, collaboration
description:
Comprehensive team features before public discovery.

**Goal:** Make team collaboration excellent before opening up to public.

**Components:**
1. Great invite flow (email, link, QR code)
2. GitHub team/org sync
3. Team profiles (avatar, role, status, bio)
4. Team activity feed
5. Internal comments on conversations
6. @mentions and notifications
7. Team settings and permissions

Discovery features (public directory, public comments, forking) come AFTER team is solid.

title: Enhanced invite flow - email, link, QR code
description:
Multiple ways to invite team members:

**Email Invite:**
- Enter email address
- Send branded email with invite link
- Track pending invites
- Resend/revoke capability

**Shareable Link:**
- Generate team invite link
- Optionally set expiration
- Usage limit option (e.g., max 10 uses)
- Revoke link

**QR Code:**
- Generate QR code for invite link
- Display in modal for scanning
- Useful for in-person onboarding

**Invite Landing Page:**
- /invite/[code] route
- Shows team name and who invited
- Sign up or sign in to join
- Confirm before joining

**Admin Controls:**
- View pending invites
- See who invited whom
- Revoke access

title: GitHub team/org sync
description:
Import team members from GitHub organization:

**Setup:**
- Connect GitHub account (OAuth)
- Select organization
- Choose: sync all members or specific teams

**Sync Behavior:**
- Import org members as team members
- Match by email or GitHub username
- Optionally auto-invite new org members
- Handle removed members (notify admin)

**Display:**
- Show GitHub avatar
- Link to GitHub profile
- Show GitHub username

**Permissions Mapping:**
- Org owner → Team admin
- Org member → Team member
- Custom mapping option

**Depends On:** ccs-zg9q (GitHub OAuth)

title: Team member profiles
description:
Rich profiles for team members:

**Profile Fields:**
- Display name
- Avatar (upload or GitHub)
- Role/title
- Bio (short)
- Status (available, busy, away)
- GitHub link
- Timezone

**Profile Page:**
- /team/[username] route
- Show recent conversations (non-private)
- Activity stats
- Contact/mention button

**Team Directory:**
- /team route
- Grid of team members
- Search/filter
- Show online status

**Settings:**
- Edit own profile
- Privacy controls (hide activity, etc.)

title: Team activity feed
description:
Real-time feed of team activity:

**Events:**
- New conversation started
- Conversation shared with team
- Comment added
- Member joined/left
- Significant milestones (100 messages, etc.)

**Feed Display:**
- /team/activity or sidebar widget
- Infinite scroll
- Filter by member, event type
- Time grouping (Today, Yesterday, etc.)

**Real-time:**
- Convex subscription for live updates
- New item indicator
- Optional sound/visual notification

**Privacy:**
- Only shows non-private conversations
- Respects member privacy settings

title: Team comments on conversations
description:
Internal team comments on shared conversations:

**Comment Types:**
- General comment (bottom of conversation)
- Anchored to specific message (click to add)
- Reply threads

**Features:**
- Markdown support
- @mentions (trigger notification)
- Edit/delete own comments
- Timestamp and author

**UI:**
- Comment icon on messages (shows count)
- Comment panel (slide-out or inline)
- Highlight commented messages

**Notifications:**
- Notify conversation owner
- Notify mentioned users
- Optional email digest

**Data Model:**
```typescript
comments: defineTable({
    conversation_id: v.id('conversations'),
    message_id: v.optional(v.id('messages')),  // null = general
    user_id: v.id('users'),
    content: v.string(),
    parent_comment_id: v.optional(v.id('comments')),
    created_at: v.number(),
    updated_at: v.optional(v.number()),
})
```

Note: This is TEAM comments first. Public comments come later.

title: @mentions and notifications
description:
Mention team members and get notified:

**Mention Syntax:**
- @username in comments
- Autocomplete dropdown when typing @

**Notification Types:**
- In-app (bell icon with count)
- Push (mobile, when implemented)
- Email (optional, digest or immediate)

**Notification Events:**
- Mentioned in comment
- Reply to your comment
- Comment on your conversation
- Team invite
- Permission request (future)

**Notification Center:**
- /notifications route
- Mark as read
- Click to navigate to source
- Clear all

**Settings:**
- Per-type enable/disable
- Email frequency (never, immediate, daily digest)
- Push preferences

title: feat-119: CLI - network retry queue
description:
CLI queues and retries failed syncs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Disconnect network
2. Have daemon try to sync
3. Check status, verify Pending queue: N items
4. Reconnect network
5. Verify queue drains

**Test status:** failing
notes: SUPERSEDED: Replaced by InvalidateSync pattern with built-in exponential backoff retry

title: Cursor editor agent support
description:
Add support for Cursor editor conversations:

**Challenge:**
Cursor stores history in SQLite, not JSONL:
- Location: ~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/workspaceStorage/[hash]/state.vscdb
- Table: aiService.prompts

**Implementation:**
1. Detect Cursor installation
2. Watch for SQLite file changes
3. Parse aiService.prompts table
4. Map to our conversation/message model
5. Handle incremental sync (track last rowid)

**Considerations:**
- SQLite file locking
- Different message format than Claude Code
- May need to poll rather than watch
- Cross-platform paths (macOS vs Linux vs Windows)

**Priority:** P1 - Cursor is popular, adds significant user base

title: Conversation view - show user name instead of 'User'

title: Fork conversation (Phase 2)
description:

- Maintains link to original

- Show fork count on original

Phase 2 - design now, implement later
Requires: understanding how to replay conversation to agent

title: Mobile Tier 2: Remote permission approval
description:
Approve/deny tool permissions from mobile:

**UI:**
- Permission card in conversation view
- Tool name, arguments preview
- Approve / Deny buttons
- Decision options (once, for session, all of type)

**Backend:**
- Daemon polls Convex for pending decisions OR
- WebSocket relay (design TBD per ccs-pdtu evolution)
- Confirmation UI after decision sent

**Notification:**
- Push when permission requested
- Tap opens conversation at permission

**Reference:** ~/src/happy/sources/components/PermissionFooter.tsx

Depends on: Bidirectional daemon communication architecture

title: React Native Mobile Client - Expo (iOS + Android)
description:
Mobile companion app for Codecast, similar to Happy Coder.

**Platform:** React Native via Expo (iOS + Android simultaneously)

**Tiers:**

**Tier 1 - MVP (Read-only + Notifications):**
- View sessions and conversations
- Push notifications for key events
- Team activity feed
- Basic search

**Tier 2 - Interactive:**
- Approve/deny permissions
- Send messages to agent
- Real-time session monitoring

**Tier 3 - Full Feature Parity:**
- Voice control (optional)
- Git status display
- Full conversation management

**Architecture:**
- Expo SDK 54+ (same as Happy)
- Convex React Native client
- Zustand for state (share patterns with web)
- Expo Push Notifications
- Expo Router for navigation

**Code Sharing:**
- Types/schemas: 100% shared with web
- API hooks: Adapted from web
- Business logic: Shared where possible
- UI: Rebuilt for RN primitives

**Reference:** ~/src/happy for patterns

title: GitHub OAuth integration
description:
Add GitHub authentication and repo connection:

**Auth:**
- GitHub OAuth as login option (in addition to email/password)
- Store GitHub access token securely
- Refresh token handling

**Repo Connection:**
- Link conversations to GitHub repos
- Detect repo from project path automatically
- Manual repo linking option

**Context Integration:**
- Fetch PR context for conversations
- Show related PRs in conversation view
- Link commits to conversations

**Publishing (Phase 2):**
- Publish agent traces as GitHub Gists
- Create PRs from conversation diffs
- Post conversation summaries to PR comments

**API:**
- GitHub App or OAuth App setup
- Scope: repo (read), user (read), gist (write)

This enables rich integration with developer workflow

Large content blocks (code diffs, tool outputs, file contents) should be collapsed by default with ability to expand.

title: Landing page - Littlebird style with install CTA
description:
